description: The octahedron photo arrangement has the correct photo ORDER, but some photos are rotated incorrectly. Rule: the top of every photo should point AWAY from the octahedron's equator. (1) Photos on the upper half of the octahedron: top of photo should point up (away from center). (2) Photos on the lower half: top of photo should point down (away from center, toward the bottom point). Scope: (1) locate the octahedron component (likely src/components/* with three.js or CSS 3D — note the THREE.WebGLRenderer reference in the user's console log suggests three.js); (2) review each face's photo and its current rotation; (3) flip/rotate any photos that don't match the rule so the top of the photo points outward from the equator. Do NOT change the photo ORDER, only orientation. Acceptance: every photo on the octahedron has its 'top edge' pointing away from the equator — top-half photos read right-side-up from above, bottom-half photos read right-side-up from below.
title: Octahedron photo orientation: top photos face up, bottom photos face down
